articles.headline: Indian and Bangladeshi killed in Saudi Arabia amid rising West Asia conflict
articles.summary: An Indian and a Bangladeshi national were killed in Al Kharj, Saudi Arabia, when a military projectile hit a residential area. The attack occurred as Israel struck oil depots in Tehran and Iran's president vowed to expand strikes on US targets. Twelve other Bangladeshis were wounded in the Al Kharj incident, highlighting the widening scope of the regional war into civilian infrastructure.

articles.headline: Kuwait reports drone attacks on international airport and social security building
articles.summary: Kuwait's defence ministry reported that fuel tanks at its international airport and a social security building were targeted by hostile drones on Sunday. In response, the national oil company announced a precautionary cut to crude production. The attacks occurred amid a wave of Iranian strikes across the region, prompting Kuwaiti forces to engage multiple hostile aerial targets.

articles.headline: US and Israel consider special forces operation to secure Iranian uranium
articles.summary: US and Israeli officials are discussing the deployment of special forces into Iran to secure its stockpile of highly enriched uranium. Concerns have grown that the material may have been moved since UN inspectors last verified its location nine months ago. President Donald Trump is reportedly weighing the option as a contingency plan to prevent Iran from achieving nuclear weapon capacity.

articles.headline: China’s Wang Yi says India and China should be partners, not rivals
articles.summary: Chinese Foreign Minister Wang Yi stated that India and China should view each other as opportunities rather than threats. Speaking at a press conference in Beijing, Wang noted that bilateral relations have improved following high-level meetings between PM Modi and President Xi Jinping. He emphasized the need for mutual trust and cooperation to benefit the rejuvenation of Asia.

articles.headline: India eyes reset in Nepal ties as Rashtriya Swatantra Party nears victory
articles.summary: New Delhi is preparing for improved relations with Nepal as Balen Shah's Rashtriya Swatantra Party (RSP) heads for a landslide general election victory. The RSP has won 120 of 165 directly elected seats. India hopes for a stable majority government following years of coalition volatility and seeks to counter previous pro-China leanings under the outgoing administration.

articles.headline: Iran selects new Supreme Leader successor amid intensifying war
articles.summary: Iran's Assembly of Experts has reportedly reached a consensus on a successor to Supreme Leader Ayatollah Khamenei, though the identity remains secret. The announcement comes as Israel strikes fuel depots in Tehran and threatens power infrastructure. Meanwhile, Gulf nations report Iranian drone attacks on civilian targets, including a desalination plant in Bahrain and buildings in Kuwait.

articles.headline: China rejects G2 governance model, calls for global multipolarity
articles.summary: Chinese Foreign Minister Wang Yi has rejected the idea of a 'G2' co-governance framework with the United States, stating that international affairs require collective efforts. He emphasized that China will not seek hegemony and that the world needs a multipolar landscape. Wang also called for India and China to view each other as partners rather than rivals to ensure Asian rejuvenation.

articles.headline: Emirates Maintains Flights Amid Ongoing West Asia Missile Threats
articles.summary: Dubai-based Emirates airline continues to operate its global schedule despite regional missile threats and air defense activity. While schools in Dubai declared an early spring break and some residents remain cautious, the city's infrastructure and commercial activity remain largely resilient. Airlines are utilizing alternative flight paths to ensure passenger safety.

articles.headline: US Treasury Secretary Signals More Relaxation for Russian Oil Deals
articles.summary: US Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent indicated that the United States might further ease sanctions on Russian oil to stabilize global energy prices and support economic stability in allied nations like India. The move is intended to reduce pressure on energy markets while maintaining rough edges in diplomatic relations with Moscow.

articles.headline: Sri Lanka Grants One-Month Visas to Stranded Iranian Nationals
articles.summary: The Sri Lankan government has announced a 30-day humanitarian visa for Iranian tourists and visitors unable to return home due to flight disruptions and conflict. The measure is intended to provide temporary relief and safety for those stuck in the country. Authorities stated they will evaluate extensions if the regional security situation does not improve.

articles.headline: China and India Must Maintain Proper Strategic Perception: Wang Yi
articles.summary: Chinese Foreign Minister Wang Yi emphasized that China and India should focus on development and mutual trust to strengthen the BRICS cooperation mechanism. Speaking on the sidelines of the National People's Congress, Wang stated that a stable relationship is beneficial to both nations and can provide new hope to the Global South amid global geopolitical volatility.

articles.headline: Iran to Select New Supreme Leader Following Killing of Khamenei
articles.summary: Iran is preparing to appoint a successor to Ayatollah Ali Khamenei following his death in an Israeli-linked strike. The Assembly of Experts is reportedly considering his 56-year-old son, Mojtaba Khamenei, as the leading candidate. The transition comes at a time of extreme regional tension and ongoing military conflict with Israel and the United States.

articles.headline: Trump Says Iran Launched Strike That Hit Girls' School
articles.summary: US President Donald Trump has alleged that Iran launched a missile strike that hit a girls' school in Minab, resulting in casualties. The Pentagon is currently investigating the claim. Trump used the incident to justify further military action against Tehran, while human rights groups expressed alarm over the impact of the escalating conflict on civilian populations.

articles.headline: Explosion Hits United States Embassy Compound in Oslo
articles.summary: A blast occurred at the US Embassy compound in Oslo, Norway, causing minor property damage but no reported injuries. Norwegian police have cordoned off the area and are investigating the incident as a potential deliberate attack. No group has yet claimed responsibility for the explosion, which comes amid heightened global tensions.

articles.headline: Trump Considers Ground Troops to Secure Iran's Uranium Stockpiles
articles.summary: President Trump stated that his administration is considering all options, including the deployment of ground troops, to secure and destroy Iran's nuclear material. The White House maintains that preventing Iran from acquiring a nuclear weapon is a top national security priority, even as critics warn that ground intervention would lead to a catastrophic regional war.

articles.headline: Bangladesh Implements Fuel Rationing as Regional War Impacts Imports
articles.summary: Bangladesh has started rationing petrol and diesel as the West Asia conflict disrupts its energy supply chain. Private vehicles are limited to two litres per transaction. The government cited a 95% reliance on oil and gas imports through the Gulf region and stated that rationing is necessary to ensure essential services and power plants remain operational.

articles.headline: Fresh MH370 Search Ends in Indian Ocean Without Clues
articles.summary: The latest high-tech search for missing Malaysia Airlines flight MH370 has concluded in the Indian Ocean without finding any wreckage. Despite using advanced autonomous underwater vehicles and covering 15,000 square kilometres, search teams found no new evidence of the plane, which disappeared in 2014. Families of the victims have called for a continued international effort.

articles.headline: War Prediction Markets Gain Traction on Stablecoin Betting Platforms
articles.summary: Global prediction markets on platforms like Polymarket are attracting significant interest following the outbreak of the Iran war. Users are betting on outcomes such as the potential fall of the Iranian regime. While these markets capture money-backed opinions, they remain controversial due to potential insider trading risks and the ethics of profiting from high-stakes global events.

articles.headline: Iran Targets US Missile Defense Radar Systems with Attack Drones
articles.summary: Iran is targeting U.S. and allied radar systems in the Middle East to degrade missile defense tracking capabilities. Strikes using inexpensive Shahed drones have hit strategic installations in Qatar, Jordan, and Kuwait. While U.S. Central Command maintains full combat capability, officials are concerned about the depletion of expensive air defense interceptors and the fragility of fixed radars.

articles.headline: Tehran Faces Trail of Destruction from Intense US-Israeli Air War
articles.summary: Airstrikes on the Iranian capital of Tehran have hit over 200 locations, including security headquarters, oil storage tanks, and sports stadiums. Over 1,200 civilians have been killed as collateral damage while the U.S. and Israel pursue a decapitation strategy against the regime. Residents describe a surreal atmosphere of paranoia as they witness the most intense bombing campaign in the city's history.

articles.headline: Rapper-Led Centrist Party Heads for Landslide Victory in Nepal
articles.summary: The Rastriya Swatantra Party (RSP), led by rapper Balendra Shah, is set for a landslide victory in Nepal's parliamentary elections. Shah defeated veteran four-time prime minister K.P. Sharma Oli in his own seat. The election follows youth-led anti-corruption protests that toppled the previous government, marking a dramatic shift in the nation's political landscape toward Gen Z-led leadership.

articles.headline: DG Shipping issues advisory for Indian seafarers in Gulf region
articles.summary: The Directorate General of Shipping has issued an review advisory for Indian seafarers in the Persian Gulf and Red Sea following the deaths of three sailors on a burning tanker. Authorities have urged continuous vigilance and high-level security measures for vessels operating in the conflict-ridden West Asian waters.

articles.headline: Trump warns of total military collapse for Iran after strikes
articles.summary: US President Trump warned Iran's leadership of a 'total end' after Israeli air strikes destroyed key military and oil facilities. Trump claimed the US does not seek regime change but warned that Iranian military assets would be wiped out if the conflict continues. Tensions remain high as both sides exchange threats.

articles.headline: Jaishankar to make statement in Parliament on ongoing West Asia crisis
articles.summary: External affairs minister S Jaishankar will address Parliament on Monday regarding the situation in West Asia and the safety of Indians in Gulf countries. The statement comes as the second half of the budget session begins. The session is expected to be stormy, with the Opposition prepared to confront the NDA government over a no-confidence motion against the Speaker and trade deals.

articles.headline: Shivakumar meets Congress high brass in Delhi amid leadership change talk
articles.summary: Karnataka Deputy CM DK Shivakumar traveled to New Delhi to meet senior Congress leaders, including Mallikarjun Kharge. The visit follows Chief Minister Siddaramaiah's statement that he would abide by the high command's decision on the state leadership. Speculation persists regarding a potential 2.5-year power-sharing agreement between the two leaders brokered by the party in 2023.

articles.headline: Telangana CM considers providing e-scooters to female college students
articles.summary: Chief Minister A Revanth Reddy announced that the Telangana government is considering distributing e-scooters to female college students to fulfill an election promise. Speaking on International Women's Day, he also detailed plans to make Hyderabad pollution-free by replacing diesel buses with electric vehicles by late 2026 and shifting high-pollution industries outside the city limits.

articles.headline: ADR report reveals only 14% of MPs in 18th Lok Sabha are women
articles.summary: An analysis by the Association for Democratic Reforms (ADR) shows that only 10% of lawmakers (MPs and MLAs) across India are women. In the current 18th Lok Sabha, women account for just 74 out of 543 members. West Bengal has the highest number of female MPs, while Uttar Pradesh leads in female MLAs. The report highlights significant gender disparity in political representation and candidate selection.

articles.headline: Punjab announces monthly cash transfer for women in state budget
articles.summary: Punjab Finance Minister Harpal Singh Cheema announced the 'Mukh Mantri Mawan Dhian Satikar Yojna' in the 2026-27 budget. The scheme will provide ₹1,000 per month to general category women and ₹1,500 to Scheduled Caste women. The government has earmarked ₹9,300 crore for the initiative, which aims to fulfill a key AAP election promise and support women's financial independence.

articles.headline: External Affairs Minister to brief Lok Sabha on West Asia conflict
articles.summary: S Jaishankar is scheduled to make an official statement in the Lok Sabha on Monday regarding the escalating situation in West Asia. The statement comes as the second half of the Budget session begins and more than 52,000 Indians have returned from the conflict zone. The opposition has demanded a full discussion on the geopolitical impact and safety of Indian citizens in the region.

articles.headline: Bihar faces leadership transition as Nitish Kumar era nears end
articles.summary: After two decades at the center of Bihar's power, Nitish Kumar's expected departure for a BJP-led transition leaves the state facing significant fiscal and social challenges. While GSDP grew nearly 12-fold under his tenure, Bihar remains among India's poorest states. The next administration must address high youth migration, a 33.7% poverty rate, and a lack of industrial employment.

articles.headline: BJD and Congress align for Odisha Rajya Sabha election seat
articles.summary: In a departure from its 'equidistance' policy, the Biju Janata Dal has sought Congress support to field a common candidate, Datteswar Hota, for the Rajya Sabha polls. The tactical tie-up aims to prevent the BJP from winning a third seat in the state. The move marks a significant shift in Odisha's political landscape following the BJD's 2024 assembly election defeat.

articles.headline: Parliament Budget session resumes with focus on LPG price hike
articles.summary: The second half of Parliament's Budget session begins with opposition parties set to corner the government over a ₹60 hike in domestic LPG prices. Other flashpoints include the West Asia crisis and a resolution seeking the removal of Speaker Om Birla. The government plans to push for the passage of the Electricity Amendment Bill and the Securities Markets Code during the session.

articles.headline: No Prior Cabinet Approval Needed for Minor Bilateral MoUs
articles.summary: The Indian government has relaxed rules for bilateral Memorandums of Understanding (MoUs) that have no financial or security implications. Such agreements will no longer require prior Cabinet approval, provided the Ministry of External Affairs clears them. This move aims to streamline administrative processes and allow greater flexibility for high-level diplomatic visits and interactions.

articles.headline: PM Modi Slams Bengal Government for 'Insulting' the President
articles.summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi criticized the Trinamool Congress government in West Bengal, accusing it of insulting President Droupadi Murmu by failing to adhere to protocol during her visit. Speaking at a rally, the PM stated that such actions reflect poorly on the state's respect for the country's constitutional head and its tribal community.

articles.headline: Parliament Reconvenes for Budget Session Amid Tensions in West Asia
articles.summary: The second half of the Parliament's Budget Session is set to begin with opposition parties expected to raise questions regarding India's diplomatic stance on the West Asia conflict. Key legislative items, including the Electricity (Amendment) Bill and discussions on Demand for Grants for various ministries, are on the agenda as the session resumes.

articles.headline: Modi slams TMC over alleged protocol row during President's visit
articles.summary: PM Narendra Modi criticized the West Bengal government for alleged protocol violations and mismanagement during President Droupadi Murmu's visit to Siliguri. The Ministry of Home Affairs has sought a detailed report from the state government by Sunday evening. Modi accused the ruling party of insulting the head of state and a tribal woman leader.

articles.headline: Bengaluru ward quota list finalized; women miss 50 percent mark
articles.summary: The final ward reservation list for Bengaluru's new five municipal corporations has been released, revealing that none of the councils met the mandatory 50% reservation for women. Out of 369 wards, only 176 have been reserved for women, falling nine short of the statutory requirement across the central, east, west, north, and south divisions.

articles.headline: Kiren Rijiju slams Congress over planned move against Speaker Om Birla
articles.summary: Union Minister Kiren Rijiju criticized the Congress for its move to seek the removal of Speaker Om Birla, calling it a subversion of democratic institutions. He stated that the NDA has full confidence in the Speaker and that the opposition's attempt would fail given the ruling coalition's majority in the House.

articles.headline: ADR report shows only 10 percent of elected representatives are women
articles.summary: An ADR report highlights a significant gender gap in Indian politics, with women making up only 10% of the 4,666 MPs and MLAs across the country. West Bengal has the highest percentage of women MPs, while several states have no women representatives at all, underscoring the challenges in achieving political parity.

articles.headline: Two Bangladeshi suspects arrested in Bengal for student leader's murder
articles.summary: West Bengal's Special Task Force arrested Faisal Karim Masud and Alamgir Hossain in Bongaon for the December 2025 murder of Bangladeshi student leader Sharif Osman Hadi. The suspects had illegally entered India via the Meghalaya border. Hadi, a spokesperson for Inqilab Moncho, was shot in Dhaka and died in a Singapore hospital. The duo was planning to return to Bangladesh when apprehended.

articles.headline: Stalker hurls acid at woman in Gurugram hospital in mistaken identity
articles.summary: A 32-year-old woman was critically injured after a stalker threw acid at her on the premises of Gurugram Civil Hospital. Police believe the attacker mistook the victim for his former partner, who was also at the hospital. The victim is receiving treatment at PGIMS Rohtak for burns to her shoulder and face. The suspect and his wife, who were reportedly searching for the ex-girlfriend, remain at large.

articles.headline: Social media influencer critical after high-speed crash on Meerut Expressway
articles.summary: A YouTuber suffered severe injuries after crashing his car at high speed on the Delhi-Meerut Expressway in Ghaziabad. The car reportedly hit a divider before flipping multiple times. The influencer was rushed to a private hospital in critical condition. Police are investigating the incident and checking for potential traffic violations or reckless driving captured on the car's dashcam.

articles.headline: Chemistry teacher arrested in Agra for manufacturing MDMA for rave parties
articles.summary: Agra police arrested Manoj Kumar, a teacher with an MSc in chemistry, for allegedly manufacturing precursor chemicals for MDMA. The chemicals were supplied to rave parties in Telangana. Authorities recovered hundreds of litres of chemical substances from his rented home. Kumar's name surfaced during a Hyderabad police investigation into a drug network involving a mathematics teacher in Jodhpur.

articles.headline: Class 9 student kills hostel mate with iron rod in Ballari
articles.summary: A 14-year-old student allegedly killed a classmate and injured eight others, including a warden, during a late-night rampage at Gurukul International School in Ballari. The accused used an iron rod to strike fellow students while they slept. The victim, Hemanth, was from Andhra Pradesh. The suspect fled the scene, and police have launched a search while investigating the school's management.

articles.headline: NCB dismantles dark web drug trafficking network 'Team Kalki', arrests two
articles.summary: The Narcotics Control Bureau busted a pan-India drug network operating on the dark web under the name 'Team Kalki'. Two individuals were arrested, and over 2,000 LSD blots, MDMA pills, and liquid MDMA were seized. The drugs were sourced from vendors in the Netherlands and Poland. The agency revealed that the network had shipped over 1,000 consignments across India using domestic parcels.

articles.headline: 1965 India-Pakistan war hero KG George passes away in Kerala
articles.summary: Lance Havildar KG George (retd), a Vir Chakra awardee for his gallantry during the 1965 India-Pakistan War, died at the age of 95 in Kottayam. Serving in the Corps of Signals, George was honored for maintaining vital communication lines under heavy enemy shelling in the Wagah sector. His family is awaiting the return of relatives from Dubai for final funeral services.

articles.headline: Uttarakhand Governor’s helicopter makes emergency landing due to technical snag
articles.summary: A helicopter carrying Uttarakhand Governor Lt Gen Gurmeet Singh (retd) made an emergency landing in Srinagar, Pauri Garhwal, on Sunday. The pilot reported a technical malfunction while en route to Gairsain for the state's budget session. No injuries were reported, and the governor later continued his journey to Gairsain by road under heavy security.

articles.headline: Over 52,000 Indians Evacuated from Gulf Since Conflict Escalation
articles.summary: The Indian government has facilitated the return of more than 52,000 citizens from the Gulf region since March 1, 2026, following heightened military activity in West Asia. Special flights and rerouted commercial services have been deployed. The Ministry of External Affairs continues to monitor the situation, advising Indians in high-risk zones to remain in touch with embassies.

articles.headline: Amit Shah Reaffirms Right of Persecuted Refugees to Indian Citizenship
articles.summary: Union Home Minister Amit Shah stated that the central government is committed to providing citizenship to refugees from minority communities in neighboring countries under the CAA. He criticized opposition parties for politicizing the issue, asserting that those fleeing religious persecution have a moral and legal right to seek refuge and a new life in India.

articles.headline: Student Dead After Peer Attack at Residential School in Ballari
articles.summary: A Class 9 student was killed and several others injured following a violent attack by a peer at a private residential school in Ballari, Karnataka. The attacker reportedly used a sharp object and an iron rod. Police have launched an investigation into the incident, and the school has been temporarily closed as authorities examine safety protocols.

articles.headline: Prime Minister Modi Greets Nari Shakti on International Women’s Day
articles.summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi extended greetings on International Women's Day, stating that the achievements of India's 'Nari Shakti' are a source of national pride. He emphasized that women's empowerment is at the core of government schemes and essential for building a 'Viksit Bharat.' Modi noted that the determination of women across fields is driving the country's transformative role.

articles.headline: Class 9 student attacks classmates in Ballari; one dead
articles.summary: A Class 9 student at a private residential school in Ballari attacked eight classmates with an iron rod, resulting in the death of one student and critical injuries to others. The attack allegedly occurred while the victims were asleep. Police have detained the minor suspect and are investigating the motive behind the assault.

articles.headline: GTRE lab searched after hoax email threat of drone-planted IEDs
articles.summary: The Gas Turbine Research Establishment in Bengaluru was thoroughly searched by security forces after a hoax email claimed 14 IEDs were planted at the facility using drones. No explosives were found, and authorities are now investigating the origin of the threat, which mentioned various militant groups.

articles.headline: Over 1200 additional Myanmar refugees found in Mizoram border village
articles.summary: Mizoram authorities discovered over 1,200 'unaccounted' refugees from Myanmar staying in a border village, bringing the district's total to over 6,000. Most refugees are reportedly fleeing conflict between the Myanmar military and ethnic armed groups. Local officials are working to register and provide aid to the new arrivals.

articles.headline: YouTuber UK07 Rider in critical condition after live-stream SUV crash
articles.summary: Popular YouTuber UK07 Rider is in critical condition after crashing his SUV into a divider on the Delhi-Meerut Expressway while live-streaming to 80,000 viewers. Police estimate the vehicle was traveling at 150-160 kmph at the time of the impact. The incident has raised concerns about the dangers of extreme vlogging.

articles.headline: PM Modi opens new Delhi Metro corridors and launches Phase V-A
articles.summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi inaugurated a 12.3km Pink Line extension and a 9.9km Magenta Line section in Delhi. He also laid the foundation for three new corridors under Phase V-A, adding 16.1km to the network. The total infrastructure push, worth ₹33,500 crore, includes Type-5 housing quarters. Modi used the event to criticize the previous AAP government's management of the capital.

articles.headline: India explores PPP mode for seven new high speed rail corridors
articles.summary: The Centre is considering Public-Private Partnership models for building at least one of seven new high-speed rail corridors, which are estimated to cost ₹16 trillion. The corridors include routes like Mumbai-Pune and Delhi-Varanasi. The move aims to increase private investment and reduce government spending. Currently, the Mumbai-Ahmedabad project is under construction with revised costs.

articles.headline: Airtel Unit Nxtra to Raise $1 Billion for Data Centre Expansion
articles.summary: Bharti Airtel's data centre arm, Nxtra Data, plans to raise $1 billion to expand its capacity from 200MW to 1GW. New investor Alpha Wave Global will reportedly commit $400 million, while existing partners Carlyle and Temasek may each contribute $300 million. The investment aims to capitalize on India's booming digital infrastructure and demand for AI-ready data storage facilities.

articles.headline: India Waives Port Procedures for Export Cargo Forced to Return
articles.summary: The Central Board of Indirect Taxes and Customs (CBIC) has waived certain re-import procedures for export cargo forced back due to the closure of the Strait of Hormuz. The relief, valid for 15 days, allows traders to bring back goods without complex paperwork, providing significant assistance to businesses impacted by the maritime disruptions in West Asia.

articles.headline: NHAI Secures Domestic Bitumen Supplies Amid Iran War Disruptions
articles.summary: The National Highways Authority of India is rushing to secure bitumen from state-run refiners like IOCL and BPCL as the Iran war disrupts imports. India imported about 35% of its bitumen requirement last year, largely from the Middle East. The disruption threatens India’s target of building 10,000 km of highways in FY27 as prices begin to rise due to global supply chain volatility.

articles.headline: Greater Bengaluru Authority to raise 1000 crore via municipal bonds
articles.summary: The GBA is preparing to revive the municipal bond model to raise Rs 1,000 crore for funding city infrastructure projects. The initiative aims to improve financial transparency and reduce dependence on government grants. This marks a return to a financing tool last used by the Bangalore Municipal Corporation in 1997.

articles.headline: Traffic violation penalties in India touch nearly 4000 crore in 2025
articles.summary: Data reveals that traffic violation fines across 34 states and UTs in India reached nearly Rs 3,700 crore in 2025. Uttar Pradesh and Maharashtra recorded the highest collections. The increase is attributed to the wider implementation of the eChallan system and enhanced surveillance using high-tech cameras.

articles.headline: MCD demolishes portion of Uttam Nagar murder accused's building
articles.summary: The Municipal Corporation of Delhi demolished parts of a building belonging to an accused in the Uttam Nagar murder case. The demolition follows a fatal fight between two families of different communities. Police confirmed one additional arrest in the case. The action is part of the ongoing fallout from the incident that led to the death of a local resident and sparked community tensions.

articles.headline: CJI says greater women representation in judiciary is key to credibility
articles.summary: Chief Justice of India Surya Kant emphasized that increasing the number of women on the bench is essential for public confidence in the justice system. Speaking at a conference for Indian women in law, he noted that women bring distinct insights into how law operates in homes and workplaces. He called for structural changes rather than relying on individual breakthroughs to achieve gender parity.

articles.headline: CBIC simplifies rules for returning export cargo due to Hormuz closure
articles.summary: The CBIC has issued new guidelines to expedite the handling of export cargo returning to Indian ports because vessels cannot transit the closed Strait of Hormuz. Consignments may be oaded without new Bills of Entry if seals are intact. The temporary measure, valid for 15 days, allows for 'Back to Town' procedures and ensures that export incentives are not wrongly disbursed for returned goods.

articles.headline: ED Seeks to Expedite Proceedings Against P. Chidambaram and Son
articles.summary: The Enforcement Directorate (ED) has moved to conclude the trial proceedings against former Finance Minister P. Chidambaram and his son Karti in the Aircel-Maxis and INX Media cases. The agency is seeking an early hearing in the Delhi High Court to resolve pending matters regarding prosecution sanctions and money laundering charges.

articles.headline: Yes Bank Executives Prepared to Settle Insider Trading Case with Sebi
articles.summary: Sixteen out of nineteen former and current Yes Bank executives have reportedly approached Sebi to settle allegations of insider trading. The regulator had issued show-cause notices for alleged lapses in sharing price-sensitive information before the bank's 2022 fundraise. Settlement terms are currently being negotiated, involving potential fines and voluntary bans from market activities.

articles.headline: Sony Faces $2.7 Billion Class Action Lawsuit by UK Users
articles.summary: A UK court has allowed a £2 billion ($2.7 billion) class-action lawsuit against Sony to proceed. The suit alleges that the company abused its dominant market position by charging a 30% commission on the PlayStation Store, leading to higher prices for consumers on digital games and in-game content. Sony has denied the allegations and intends to contest the case.

articles.headline: Women Face Ongoing Barriers in Claiming Property Inheritance Rights
articles.summary: Despite 2005 legal amendments granting equal inheritance rights, women in India own only 18.7% of homes independently. Social pressure often forces daughters to sign 'relinquishment deeds' in favor of brothers. Legal experts emphasize the importance of writing wills and family settlement agreements to prevent lengthy court battles and ensure women can exercise their financial rights.

articles.headline: Court orders Air India to pay 17000 for damaged baggage
articles.summary: A consumer court in Bengaluru has directed Air India to pay Rs 17,000 in compensation and legal costs to a passenger whose strolley bag was delivered in a damaged condition. The court ruled that the airline's failure to provide a damage report or resolve the complaint constituted a deficiency in service.

articles.headline: Bengaluru homebuyers face legal maze over illegally mortgaged apartment land
articles.summary: Nearly 400 homebuyers in a Bengaluru apartment complex have discovered their land was illegally mortgaged to a private firm by developers for a Rs 28 crore loan. The residents are now fighting a legal battle as the financial firm has initiated recovery proceedings under the SARFAESI Act, leaving owners in financial uncertainty.

articles.headline: Chhattisgarh HC rules against arbitrary blacklisting of contractors
articles.summary: The Chhattisgarh High Court set aside a three-year ban on contractors, stating that blacklisting has serious 'civil death' consequences for a business. The court ruled that such measures must only be taken after a clear finding of deliberate wrongdoing and through a process that satisfies principles of natural justice.

articles.headline: CJI Surya Kant highlights low representation of women in higher judiciary
articles.summary: Chief Justice of India Surya Kant noted that only 15% of High Court judges in India are women, calling for more equal participation in the judiciary. Out of 781 judges across 25 HCs, only 116 are women. The CJI emphasized that achieving gender parity is essential for a more diverse and fair judicial system.

articles.headline: HT Analysis finds significant contamination in Delhi tap water samples
articles.summary: A week-long HT investigation revealed that eight out of 18 water samples collected across Delhi were unfit for consumption. Tests conducted by a government-certified lab found high levels of coliform bacteria, indicating sewage contamination. Areas like Kamla Nagar and Chittaranjan Park showed significant impurities, contradicting official claims about the safety of the city's piped water.

articles.headline: Semaglutide generics launch in India may raise therapy dropout risks
articles.summary: As over 50 semaglutide generics enter the Indian market this month, experts warn that expanding prescriptions to general practitioners requires stricter patient profiling. Branded generics are expected to be priced at one-third of innovator drugs, potentially tripling monthly sales volumes. However, adequate monitoring is essential to prevent dropouts from weight-loss and diabetes therapies.

articles.headline: Cipla Recalls 400 Cartons of Generic Cancer Drug in United States
articles.summary: Cipla's US subsidiary has voluntarily recalled 400 cartons of its generic anti-cancer drug, Nilotinib Capsules, due to a manufacturing issue. The recall was initiated after the US FDA identified potential defects in the packaging that could affect the medication's efficacy. The company stated it is working closely with regulators to resolve the matter.

articles.headline: India Weighs Tighter Rules to Curb Nicotine Lozenge Access for Minors
articles.summary: The government plans to move 2 mg nicotine lozenges from general stores to regulated pharmacy shelves. The proposal, recommended by the Drugs Technical Advisory Board, aims to prevent easy access for minors and non-smokers. If implemented, retailers will require a formal drug sale license to sell these products, which were previously exempt under Schedule K of the Drugs Rules.

articles.headline: Hospitals must strictly maintain medico-legal case records says Bengaluru body
articles.summary: The Bruhat Bengaluru Mahanagara Palike has issued a directive to all hospitals to maintain comprehensive records of medico-legal cases. This follows reports of hospitals failing to provide crucial data for police investigations. The rule aims to streamline evidence gathering in cases of accidents, assaults, and domestic violence.

articles.headline: Tata study shows ultra-low cancer drug dose reduces costs by 90 percent
articles.summary: Research by Tata Memorial Hospital indicates that using just one-tenth the standard dose of the immunotherapy drug nivolumab is effective for treating various cancers. This approach could reduce the monthly treatment cost from Rs 2.4 lakh to approximately Rs 18,000, making life-saving therapy accessible to many more patients.

articles.headline: Barcelona Secures Hard-Fought Win Over Athletic Bilbao
articles.summary: Lamine Yamal's spectacular long-range goal earned Barcelona a 1-0 victory over Athletic Bilbao in La Liga. The 18-year-old forward's strike in the 78th minute broke the deadlock in a closely contested match. The win keeps Barcelona at the top of the table, while Bilbao remains in fifth place after their first home defeat of the season.

articles.headline: Karnataka hotel body warns of menu price hikes due to LPG costs
articles.summary: The Bengaluru Bruhat Hotels Association warned that food prices may rise following a hike in commercial LPG cylinder prices. Commercial cylinders became costlier by ₹114.5, while domestic rates rose by ₹60. Combined with rising costs for oil and coffee beans, the industry faces significant pressure. CM Siddaramaiah criticized the Union government, stating the hike burdens the middle class.

articles.headline: Fino Payments Bank CEO arrest triggers scrutiny of merchant sourcing networks
articles.summary: The arrest of Fino Payments Bank MD Rishi Gupta over alleged GST evasion by merchant-sourcing partners has raised questions about financial institutions' liability for client actions. The probe relates to transactions in the real-money gaming segment. The RBI has approved Ketan Merchant as interim CEO for three months while the bank continues its transition into a small finance bank.

articles.headline: Indian CDMOs expand capacity to meet global demand for complex drugs
articles.summary: Indian contract development and manufacturing organisations are increasing investments in technologies like peptides and antibody-drug conjugates. Sai Life Sciences aims to triple its capacity to 2,000 cubic metres, while Akums Drugs is investing over ₹450 crore in new manufacturing blocks. The shift reflects global pharmaceutical companies outsourcing high-value drug development to India.

articles.headline: Dilip Buildcon aims to be net debt-free by end of FY28
articles.summary: Infrastructure firm Dilip Buildcon plans to achieve a near net debt-free status by FY28. The company intends to use cash flows from its EPC vertical, mining business, and asset monetization through InvITs to reduce its current debt of ₹2,150 crore. The firm has successfully reduced its net debt from ₹3,387 crore in FY19 despite recent fluctuations.

articles.headline: Indian pharmaceutical market grows 11 percent in February
articles.summary: The Indian pharmaceutical market recorded 11% growth in February, driven by anti-diabetic, cardiac, and anti-infective therapies. New product introductions and price adjustments contributed to the total turnover of over ₹2.44 trillion for the moving annual turnover ending February 2026. Experts noted healthy volume growth in the domestic market over the last quarter.

articles.headline: New fraud framework to offer compensation for digital payment scams
articles.summary: The RBI has proposed a new framework to limit customer liability in digital transactions, offering compensation for frauds up to ₹50,000. Customers could receive up to 85% of their loss if reported promptly. The move aims to boost consumer confidence as card and internet frauds accounted for nearly 67% of total reported incidents in the 2024-25 financial year.

articles.headline: Bharat Forge stock hits all-time high on global truck recovery
articles.summary: Shares of Bharat Forge surged 24% in a month as North American Class 8 truck orders jumped 159% year-on-year. The company is also benefiting from rising domestic commercial vehicle demand and a robust ₹11,100 crore defense order book. Analysts expect the defense segment to deliver 30-40% revenue growth in FY27 as global military spending increases due to ongoing conflicts.

articles.headline: Small towns surpass major cities in mutual fund investor folios
articles.summary: For the first time, investors from smaller towns (B-30) have overtaken the top 30 cities in their share of mutual fund folios. B-30 locations accounted for 50.1% of total individual investor folios in January 2026. This shift reflects growing financial awareness and the success of investor education campaigns in expanding market penetration beyond India's traditional urban centers.

articles.headline: First IPO at GIFT City IFSC opens for offshore capital
articles.summary: XED Institute has launched the first IPO at the GIFT City IFSC, seeking to raise $12 million. The offering is being closely watched as a test for the fledgling exchange's ability to attract dollar-denominated capital. Proponents say the platform offers tax efficiency and faster regulatory approvals for smaller firms compared to traditional domestic listings in India.

articles.headline: MFI credit costs spike to 15 percent amid rising defaults
articles.summary: Microfinance institutions are facing sharp increases in credit costs, rising to 15.5% in late 2025 due to higher risk provisions and write-offs. The industry is seeing a decline in active loans and total portfolio size. Experts warn that political factors, including potential loan waivers and restrictive state legislations, could further delay the sector's recovery.

articles.headline: Boeing Cites Geopolitics as the Biggest Risk to Global Aviation
articles.summary: Boeing vice president Brendan Nelson identified regional conflicts in West Asia and Eastern Europe as the primary threats to the aviation industry. Prolonged warfare has led to rerouting, higher fuel costs, and reduced demand in certain corridors. Despite these risks, Boeing remains committed to its Indian supply chain, sourcing $1 billion in products annually from over 300 suppliers.

articles.headline: Chinese Electronics Firms Tap Parent Companies for Support Amid Challenges
articles.summary: Chinese smartphone and electronics makers in India, including Xiaomi, Vivo, and Oppo, are increasingly relying on parent companies for financial support through bank guarantees and loyalty settlements. Filings show significant related-party transactions and royalty payments as they navigate regulatory scrutiny and legal challenges in the Indian market.

articles.headline: Househelp Apps Top 2 Million Monthly Orders Amid Profitability Questions
articles.summary: On-demand domestic service platforms like Urban Company and Snabbit have collectively surpassed 2 million monthly orders. While the sector is expanding rapidly into new cities, concerns remain over high burn rates and the path to profitability. The segment is seeing increased competition from quick-commerce giants entering the home services market.

articles.headline: Delhi's Khan Market Rents Rise 8% Amid High Demand
articles.summary: Retail rents in Delhi's Khan Market have increased by 8% year-on-year, driven by limited supply and high demand from premium brands. According to Cushman & Wakefield, the monthly rent reached approximately ₹1,700-1,800 per square foot. Other high-street locations in the NCR, including Gurugram's DLF Galleria, also saw significant rent hikes.

articles.headline: Consumer Goods Companies See Sales Surge During T20 World Cup
articles.summary: Manufacturers of consumer electronics, apparel, and snacks reported a 30-35% spike in sales during the recently concluded T20 World Cup. Quick-commerce platforms saw record order volumes on match days, particularly for beverages and snacks. Official merchandise sales for Team India also reached new highs, with jerseys selling out in several major cities.

articles.headline: KKR Considers Sale of Data Centre Cooling Specialist Firm
articles.summary: Private equity giant KKR is reportedly exploring the sale of its portfolio company, CoolIT Systems, which specializes in liquid cooling for data centres. The deal could value the company at over $3 billion, reflecting the surging demand for advanced cooling solutions as AI-driven data centres generate unprecedented levels of heat.

articles.headline: ACME Solar Raises ₹2,300 Crore from NaBFID for Debt Refinancing
articles.summary: Renewable energy firm ACME Solar has secured ₹2,300 crore in long-term financing from the National Bank for Financing Infrastructure and Development (NaBFID). The funds will be used to refinance existing debt for its solar power projects across India. This represents one of the largest debt-refinancing deals in the Indian green energy sector this year.

articles.headline: SDAL Invests $1.4 Billion in DeepTech Defence Manufacturing Facility
articles.summary: Solar Defence and Aerospace (SDAL) has announced a $1.4 billion investment to set up a deep-tech manufacturing hub in Nagpur. The facility will focus on producing advanced unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs), counter-drone systems, and AI-based surveillance equipment. The project is expected to generate thousands of high-tech jobs and boost India's self-reliance in defense.

articles.headline: Sembcorp and Hexa Bidding for Enel’s Indian Green Energy Unit
articles.summary: Sembcorp Industries and Hexa Climate Solutions are competing to acquire Enel Group’s Indian renewable energy business. The deal has an estimated enterprise value of $300 million and includes 760MW of operational wind and solar assets along with a 2.5GW development pipeline. The sale process, managed by HSBC, follows a previous deal with Waaree Energies that fell through.

articles.headline: New SEBI Fee Rules to Drive Consolidation Among Small Agents
articles.summary: Revised Total Expense Ratio (TER) limits by SEBI are expected to impact small mutual fund distributors. Industry experts predict these agents will be forced to partner with larger platforms to manage increased compliance and the removal of GST from the base expense ratio. Currently, about 90% of distributors are non-GST compliant and may see lower commission payouts.

articles.headline: Auto Component Makers Focus on Powertrain-Agnostic Products for Green Shift
articles.summary: Suppliers like Greaves Cotton, Tenneco, and Belrise Industries are pitching parts that work across electric, hybrid, CNG, and internal combustion engines. This strategy addresses global uncertainty in technology adoption as carmakers slow EV plans. Agnostic portfolios allow suppliers to remain relevant regardless of which technology dominates the future fuel mix.

articles.headline: Warren Buffett Steps Down as Berkshire Hathaway CEO After 60 Years
articles.summary: Warren Buffett has officially stepped down as CEO of Berkshire Hathaway, passing leadership to Greg Abel. Abel inherits a conglomerate valued at over $1 trillion with a cash reserve of $373 billion. While pledgeing to maintain Buffett’s conservative values, Abel faces pressure to deploy the massive cash pile as the company faces a drop in operating earnings and recent market underperformance.

articles.headline: Zepto Targets April IPO Filing Amid Valuation Scrutiny
articles.summary: Quick-commerce startup Zepto plans to file updated IPO documents with Sebi by late April. The company is currently holding preliminary talks with domestic mutual funds. However, its previous $7-billion private valuation is under review, with potential public market debut estimates adjusted down by 15-20% to a range of $5.6 billion to $5.95 billion based on current unit economics.

articles.headline: Bertelsmann India Investments Targets Startups Focused on Rural 'Bharat'
articles.summary: Venture capital firm Bertelsmann India Investments is doubling down on startups that help rural villages earn or spend money. The firm sees rural India as a largely untapped frontier due to infrastructure gaps. BII has recently invested in Rozana and AgroStar, distinguishing 'Bharat' as a market where creating distribution is more vital than traditional marketing strategies.

articles.headline: Middle East Conflict Threatens L&T’s International Order Execution
articles.summary: Larsen & Toubro shares have fallen 8% as investors worry about the company’s heavy exposure to the Middle East. The region accounts for 75.5% of L&T’s international order book and 34% of its consolidated revenues. Analysts warn of potential execution delays, order deferments, and margin pressure if regional hostilities prolong and affect real estate and hydrocarbon projects.

articles.headline: Tata Power Aims to Restart Mundra Plant as Energy Demand Rises
articles.summary: Tata Power expects to restart its 4,000-MW Mundra thermal plant by the end of March. The facility has been shut since July 2025 due to coal cost disputes with client states. Rising summer power demand and soaring global LNG prices have improved the company's negotiating position. Higher coal prices would also benefit Tata Power through its 30% stake in an Indonesian coal mine.

articles.headline: Bold Care Shifts Marketing Focus Toward Brand Awareness Over Performance
articles.summary: Sexual wellness brand Bold Care is pivoting its strategy to focus on brand awareness rather than expensive performance marketing. Co-founder Rajat Jadhav stated that creative campaigns featuring celebrities like Ranveer Singh and influencers like Samay Raina have driven significant organic engagement. The brand aims for long-term category creation in the online sexual health market.

articles.headline: Leading AC Makers Hike Prices by 5-15% Ahead of Summer
articles.summary: Leading room air-conditioner manufacturers including Daikin, Voltas, and Blue Star are raising prices by 5-15% between February and April. The hikes are driven by rising raw material costs for copper, a record-high US dollar, and increased freight charges. Despite the higher prices, the industry expects strong sales growth of at least 15% due to forecasts of a hotter summer.

articles.headline: Saudi Aramco Shares Surge as Iran War Disrupts Global Oil Supply
articles.summary: Shares of Saudi Aramco climbed nearly 5% as global oil prices topped $90 a barrel. The jump comes as the Iran war enters its second week, leading to production cuts in the UAE and Kuwait and the closure of the Strait of Hormuz. Analysts believe Aramco can offset potential export volume declines by re-routing shipments to Red Sea facilities to maintain energy exports.

articles.headline: Lloyds Metals Bets on Congo Copper Mining Expansion
articles.summary: Lloyds Metals and Energy Ltd is expanding into copper mining in the Katanga region of the Democratic Republic of Congo. Managing director Rajesh Gupta believes the company can replicate its success in India's Gadchiroli region despite Congo's political and regulatory risks. The move targets surging global copper demand for electrification, though execution risks in the region remain high.

articles.headline: Reliance Strategy Focuses on Acquiring Regional Legacy FMCG Brands
articles.summary: Reliance Consumer Products is building its FMCG business by acquiring established regional brands like Manna and Udhaiyam. By integrating these local labels into Reliance's massive distribution network, the company aims to quickly compete with giants like Hindustan Unilever. This strategy differs from rivals who primarily focus on acquiring digital-first direct-to-consumer startups.

articles.headline: Indian Food Delivery Giants Regain Growth Momentum in Q3
articles.summary: Swiggy, Zomato, and magicpin reported a recovery in growth during the October-December quarter, driven by festive demand and affordable offerings. magicpin saw over 60% improvement in unit economics at the order level. Swiggy’s food delivery GOV grew 20.5% year-on-year, while Zomato reported 21.3% growth in GOV, indicating strong consumer adoption across major Indian markets.

articles.headline: Producers Include Mandatory Promotion Clauses in Movie Star Contracts
articles.summary: The film industry is increasingly making promotional appearances a contractual obligation for lead actors. A recent dispute in the Malayalam industry involving actor Biju Menon highlights this shift. Producers estimate that 20-30% of marketing budgets rely on star visibility. Legal experts say participation is now a negotiated term rather than a courtesy to ensure commercial success.

articles.headline: Jaquar Group Targets ₹1,700 Crore Lighting Revenue in Three Years
articles.summary: Bathroom and lighting solutions provider Jaquar Group is aiming to scale its lighting business to ₹1,700 crore over the next three years. The company plans to expand its portfolio in architectural, outdoor, and landscape lighting. Jaquar Lighting currently contributes 8-10% to the group’s total revenue and sees significant growth potential in both commercial and consumer segments.

articles.headline: Euler Motors Calls for Easing Auto PLI Scheme Thresholds
articles.summary: Electric vehicle startup Euler Motors has urged the government to relax eligibility criteria for the Auto PLI scheme. Founder Saurav Kumar stated that the high minimum revenue threshold of ₹10,000 crore prevents innovative startups from participating. Kumar argues that easing these norms would incentivize startups that are pushing R&D and technology at the forefront of green mobility.

articles.headline: Swiggy Instamart Launches 'Noice' Private Label to Boost Margins
articles.summary: Swiggy Instamart has introduced a premium food and beverage private label called 'Noice' featuring bold packaging and regional snacks. By leveraging hyperlocal data and focusing on high-frequency snack categories like banana chips and dosa batter, the firm aims to improve unit economics. The strategy focuses on discovery and premium pricing rather than the traditional low-cost staple model.

articles.headline: Layoff Memes Changing How Employees Process Job Loss Uncertainty
articles.summary: The rise of viral memes about layoffs, such as those following Block’s recent staff cuts, is altering workplace perceptions. While humor acts as a coping mechanism, psychologists warn that constant exposure to layoff news as comedy can lead to chronic uncertainty and desensitization. The trend marks a shift from historical stigmas toward a more open, yet anxious, digital culture of job loss.

articles.headline: Swizzle Co-Founder Vrinda Singhal on Mentorship and Slow Workday Starts
articles.summary: Vrinda Singhal, co-founder of the mocktail brand Swizzle, highlights the importance of 'ruthless prioritization' and engineering-led structural thinking in running a startup. Singhal advocates for slow morning routines, including quiet time and walks, to maintain productivity and prevent burnout. The Bengaluru-based entrepreneur focuses on quality control in the clean-label beverage market.

articles.headline: Exporters worried as shipping dumping at ports causes logistical bottlenecks
articles.summary: Indian exporters are raising concerns about the 'dumping' of goods at ports due to logistical blocks in the Persian Gulf. The congestion is impacting supply chains and increasing shipping costs. The government is monitoring the situation and considering measures to streamline customs and port clearances.

articles.headline: Guruvayur Devaswom Board appoints first woman member in five decades
articles.summary: MU Shinija has been appointed to the nine-member Guruvayur Devaswom Board, becoming the first woman to serve on the temple panel since rules were amended in 1978. Nominated as a CPI representative, her appointment on International Women's Day was hailed by the board chairman as a historic milestone. The board manages the famed Sree Krishna Temple in Guruvayur, Kerala.

articles.headline: Kerala government extends wild boar culling order for one more year
articles.summary: The Kerala Forest Department has extended its order permitting the controlled culling of wild boars for another year. Local self-government officials and authorized hunters can kill boars that stray into human habitations and cause damage to property or crops. The decision followed recommendations to mitigate threats to life and agriculture in several parts of the state.

articles.headline: Conflict in Middle East Threatens Global Fertiliser Supply and Prices
articles.summary: The ongoing regional war in the Middle East has disrupted the production and export of nitrogen-based fertilisers, raising fears of global food price hikes. Nations reliant on imports from the Persian Gulf, including India, are facing supply shortages. Industry experts warn that prolonged conflict could significantly impact the upcoming planting season and agricultural yields.

articles.headline: Karnataka coffee growers explore climate-resilient Liberica and Excelsa varieties
articles.summary: With traditional Arabica and Robusta crops struggling due to erratic weather patterns, coffee planters in Karnataka are turning to more resilient varieties like Liberica and Excelsa. Experts believe these species, which can better withstand heat and drought, may be the future of the industry as climate change impacts traditional yields.

articles.headline: Wildlife cameras capture elephants using first NH-209 overpass in Bengaluru
articles.summary: Elephants were officially recorded using the state's first dedicated wildlife overpass on NH-209 near Tataguni for the first time. The structure was built at a cost of Rs 20 crore to provide a safe crossing corridor for animals moving between Bannerghatta National Park and Savandurga forest, reducing roadkill risks.

articles.headline: OpenAI executive resigns over military and surveillance deal with Pentagon
articles.summary: OpenAI's top robotics executive, Caitlin Kalinowski, resigned in protest of the company's deal with the US government. She cited concerns over the deployment of AI for war and domestic surveillance without judicial oversight. The resignation follows a similar move at rival Anthropic and highlights growing ethical tensions regarding the military use of artificial intelligence technology.

articles.headline: AI technology emerges as major demand driver for India's office market
articles.summary: India's office market is seeing a surge in demand driven by artificial intelligence firms. Global tech giants and AI startups are actively leasing mid-to-large spaces in hubs like Bengaluru, Hyderabad, and Pune. Flex workspace operators report numerous deals as GCCs shift focus toward machine learning and data science, contributing to the 75.2 million square feet absorbed in 2025.

articles.headline: AI could create $150 billion value for manufacturing MSMEs by 2035
articles.summary: A study by PwC India and ORF suggests that artificial intelligence could contribute up to $149.9 billion in value to Indian manufacturing MSMEs by 2035. Deployed as a co-pilot, AI can help small enterprises overcome low productivity traps and compete on innovation and speed. The report emphasizes that AI will strengthen supply chain resilience rather than simply replacing human labor.

articles.headline: Data centre demand in India to rise with digital services growth
articles.summary: Schneider Electric officials predict that data centre demand in India will continue to grow as digital services and smart cities expand. Although India generates 20% of global data, only 2-3% is processed domestically. Total capacity is expected to reach 7-8 GW from the current 2 GW. Efficient energy management is required as data centres could eventually consume 4% of global power.

articles.headline: AWS and MS Azure Reroute West Asia Data Workloads to India
articles.summary: Hyperscale cloud providers including Amazon Web Services and Microsoft Azure are rerouting data centre workloads from West Asia to India due to regional conflict. Services in Dubai, Abu Dhabi, and Oman are being shifted to hubs in Mumbai, Hyderabad, and Chennai. The move prioritizes banking and critical workloads to minimize latency and ensure continuity during network disruptions.

articles.headline: DoT May Stick to 5% Spectrum Fee for Satcom Players
articles.summary: The Department of Telecommunications (DoT) is likely to maintain a 5% adjusted gross revenue (AGR) fee for satellite communication (satcom) spectrum, rejecting regulator Trai's proposal for a lower 4% fee. However, the government may offer a 1% discount for companies providing services in hard-to-connect rural areas to encourage digital inclusion across the country.

articles.headline: AI Software May Impact 6-20% of IT Jobs Over 18 Months
articles.summary: Generative AI and automated productivity tools could affect up to 20% of roles in the Indian IT services sector over the next year and a half. Experts suggest that while AI will create new roles in orchestration and ethics, traditional coding and maintenance jobs are most vulnerable. Companies are being urged to reskill employees to handle higher-value tasks.

articles.headline: X Probes Offensive Posts Generated by Grok AI Chatbot
articles.summary: Elon Musk's social media platform X is investigating reports of offensive and sexually explicit content being generated by its Grok AI chatbot. The probe comes after several users shared screenshots of inappropriate responses. Regulators in multiple countries are monitoring the situation, raising concerns over the safety guardrails in AI systems.

articles.headline: OpenAI Head of Robotics Resigns Over Company's Pentagon Deal
articles.summary: The head of robotics at OpenAI has resigned, citing ethical concerns over the company's new contract with the US Department of Defense. The deal reportedly involves developing AI models for military logistics and surveillance. The resignation highlights growing internal tension within AI firms regarding the use of their technology for lethal and military applications.

articles.headline: Hotel Chains Deploy AI for Efficiency While Retaining Human Staff
articles.summary: Major hotel chains like Radisson and Roseate are integrating AI for energy management, guest preference tracking, and automated check-ins. While AI-driven robots and chatbots are helping optimize costs by up to 25%, industry leaders emphasize that the 'human element' remains essential for primary hospitality services. The technology currently focuses on backend operations and energy saving.

articles.headline: OpenAI Robotics Head Resigns in Protest of Pentagon Military Deal
articles.summary: Caitlin Kalinowski, head of OpenAI’s robotics team, has resigned following the company’s agreement to deploy AI models within the Pentagon’s classified network. Kalinowski cited concerns over domestic surveillance and autonomous weapons as her reason for leaving. OpenAI defended the deal as a workable path for responsible national security use while maintaining specific red lines.

articles.headline: OpenAI-Anthropic Rivalry Threatens Consensus on AI Safety Standards
articles.summary: The growing feud between Sam Altman of OpenAI and Dario Amodei of Anthropic is creating friction over AI regulation and safety. The rivalry has intensified as both firms navigate dealings with the Pentagon. While Anthropic rejected certain military requests on safety grounds, OpenAI secured a network deal, fueling distrust that could fragment public debate on how AI technology develops.

articles.headline: Karnataka government allocates 10 crore for quantum technology roadmap
articles.summary: The state government has set aside Rs 10 crore to develop a 'Quantum Roadmap' for 2026-27. The project aims to position Karnataka as a global hub for quantum research by building human resource capacity and supporting a local ecosystem of startups and research institutions in the emerging high-tech field.

articles.headline: Data breach at Cognizant subsidiary exposes 3.4 million people
articles.summary: Trizetto Provider Solutions, a unit of Cognizant, reported a massive data breach affecting 3.4 million individuals. The breach potentially exposed names, social security numbers, and health insurance details. The company has notified affected parties and is working with law enforcement to investigate the hack.

articles.headline: US waiver allows India to import stranded Russian crude oil
articles.summary: The US has granted a 30-day waiver allowing Indian refiners to import stranded crude oil from sanctioned Russian firms Lukoil and Rosneft. The move aims to stabilize global energy markets as conflict in West Asia disrupts traditional supplies. India is also exploring alternative routes for LPG and LNG that bypass the Strait of Hormuz to ensure energy security amid the ongoing regional war.

articles.headline: Union ministries must spend 26 percent of budget in two months
articles.summary: Government data shows Union ministries need to spend an average of 26% of their revised budget estimates during February and March. Some departments, like Corporate Affairs and Jal Shakti, have much larger gaps of 93% and 72% respectively. Guidelines require spending to be limited to 33% in the final quarter, but historical trends show significant undershooting in several major ministries.

articles.headline: Over 40,000 Indian export containers stranded due to West Asia war
articles.summary: Logistics firms report that 40,000 to 45,000 Indian export containers are currently stranded in transit or at international ports due to the West Asia crisis. Cargo worth up to $1.5 billion faces diversion or return to India, leading to a five-fold increase in costs. Basmati rice exporters are particularly affected, with 4 lakh tonnes of produce currently caught in the shipping disruptions.

articles.headline: India's strategic oil reserves provide limited cover as shipments slow
articles.summary: India's strategic petroleum reserves (SPR) currently hold only five to six days of consumption cover as West Asian shipping through the Hormuz Strait halts. While commercial stocks add eight weeks of buffer, these are operational and require constant replenishment. No crude loadings for India have occurred in March from Gulf countries, forcing a search for alternative Russian and African grades.

articles.headline: Centre assures smooth gas supply for fertiliser production during crisis
articles.summary: The government is working on plans to mitigate the 40% shortfall in LNG supplies from the Middle East to ensure domestic fertiliser production. Officials have met with leading companies to monitor urea and DAP stocks. Although West Asian urea prices have surged to $600 per tonne, India currently holds 17.73 million tonnes of fertiliser stocks, which is 36% more than last year.

articles.headline: West Asia conflict delays Indian labor migration to the Gulf
articles.summary: Recruitment for the Gulf Cooperation Council (GCC) has slowed as regional tensions stall job placements for an estimated 300,000 Indians. While over 9 million Indians work in the Gulf, current security concerns have forced a pause in hiring for infrastructure and energy projects. Some regional governments have eased overstay rules for workers unable to leave due to flight disruptions.

articles.headline: Foreign investors withdraw ₹21,000 crore from Indian equities in four days
articles.summary: Foreign portfolio investors (FPIs) sold Indian stocks worth ₹21,000 crore over the last four trading sessions as global risk sentiment soured due to the West Asia war. The selloff follows a month of high inflows in February. Analysts attribute the reversal to geopolitical uncertainty, market corrections, and the depreciation of the rupee against the US dollar.

articles.headline: States utilize only 52 percent of annual capex budget till January
articles.summary: An analysis of 23 states shows they have spent just ₹5.38 trillion of their ₹10.37 trillion budgeted capital expenditure for FY26. Telangana is the only state to exceed its target, while West Bengal and Uttar Pradesh remain among the lowest spenders. Subdued state spending contrasts with the Central government, which has reached nearly 77% of its revised capex estimates.

articles.headline: RBI announces ₹1 trillion bond purchase to manage liquidity pressure
articles.summary: The Reserve Bank of India will conduct open-market operations worth ₹1 trillion in two tranches to address liquidity deficits caused by advance tax and GST outflows. Durable liquidity has also been strained by interventions in the forex market to stabilize the rupee. Market yields on 10-year bonds have hardened as participants weigh global risk factors and domestic supply pressures.

articles.headline: Government forms committee to draft comprehensive SEZ 2.0 policy
articles.summary: A 17-member committee has been established to suggest reforms for India's special economic zones. The panel will submit a roadmap within six months for 'SEZ 2.0', focusing on harmonizing various export promotion schemes. The group includes representatives from NITI Aayog, Commerce, and Customs to create a broad-based framework for domestic manufacturing and export operations.

articles.headline: US Treasury Secretary thanks international partners for energy market stability
articles.summary: Scott Bessent has expressed gratitude to international partners for maintaining energy market stability following his comments on 'permitting' India to buy Russian oil. The US issued a 30-day waiver for stranded Russian crude to alleviate pressure from the West Asia conflict. New Delhi maintains that its oil purchases serve global interests by keeping prices in check during regional wars.

articles.headline: US offers $20 billion reinsurance plan for Persian Gulf shipping
articles.summary: The Trump administration has announced a $20 billion maritime reinsurance program to revive commercial traffic in the Strait of Hormuz. The plan aims to provide affordable war-risk insurance for vessels after private insurers canceled coverage due to Iranian attacks. The move follows a surge in global oil prices and a near-total halt in energy shipments through the strategic waterway.

articles.headline: Investors advised to avoid panic selling as West Asia war continues
articles.summary: Financial experts recommend that households maintain their SIPs and avoid knee-jerk reactions to market volatility triggered by the Iran war. Historical data suggests that war-related selloffs are transitory, with markets recovering once earnings stabilize. Investors are advised to maintain a 10-15% hedge in gold and silver and postpone major discretionary spending during the crisis.

articles.headline: India's trade deals with EU and US to boost manufacturing
articles.summary: India's recent trade agreements with the European Union, UK, and US are expected to provide domestic exporters with tariff parity against rivals like Vietnam and Bangladesh. The deals aim to secure market access for labor-intensive sectors and attract $100 billion in investment from EFTA nations. However, exporters must navigate non-tariff barriers like the EU's carbon border tax.

articles.headline: Foreign Banks' Credit Card Base Shrinks as Local Rivals Expand
articles.summary: Foreign lenders in India, including American Express and Standard Chartered, have seen their credit card market share decline as domestic banks aggressively scale. Over the past year, foreign banks' outstanding credit card base dropped by 6% YoY. Meanwhile, Indian public and private sector banks are leveraging better digital infrastructure and broader reach to capture new users.

articles.headline: AC Prices Jump as Manufacturers Hike Rates Ahead of Summer
articles.summary: Air conditioner manufacturers including Daikin, Voltas, and LG have increased prices by 3-5% ahead of the peak summer season. The price hike is attributed to rising raw material costs for copper and aluminum, as well as higher logistics expenses. Despite the increase, companies expect robust sales due to forecasts of an exceptionally hot summer across India.

articles.headline: FPIs Shift Investment from IT to Finance and Capex Sectors
articles.summary: Foreign Portfolio Investors (FPIs) have pulled over ₹8,500 crore out of the Indian IT sector, citing concerns over AI-led disruptions and global slowdown. Instead, FPIs are increasing their stakes in financial services, automobiles, and capital goods sectors, which are seen as better positioned to benefit from domestic economic growth and government spending.

articles.headline: US-Israel-Iran Conflict Puts Emerging Markets Recovery at High Risk
articles.summary: Escalating military conflict in West Asia has triggered volatility in emerging markets, with rising oil prices and a stronger dollar threatening economies reliant on energy imports. Wall Street analysts warn that a prolonged war could derail the global recovery, prompting investors to shift capital back to safe-haven assets in the United States and Europe.

articles.headline: Public Sector Banks Plan Green Bond Issues Following BoB Success
articles.summary: Major state-run lenders, including Union Bank and Punjab National Bank, are planning to issue green bonds worth up to ₹10,000 crore following the successful ₹5,000 crore issue by Bank of Baroda. The capital will be used to fund sustainable infrastructure and renewable energy projects, aligning with India's long-term carbon emission reduction goals.

articles.headline: India Raises LPG Prices as Hormuz Crisis Chokes Energy Flows
articles.summary: The Indian government has announced an increase in domestic LPG cylinder prices by ₹50 to ₹100, citing a sharp rise in international gas costs due to the blockade of the Strait of Hormuz. With over 90% of India's LPG imports passing through the strait, the disruption has forced the government to pass some of the cost burden to consumers while seeking alternative suppliers.

articles.headline: Bulls Avoid Buying the Dip Amid Escalating West Asia Conflict
articles.summary: Indian stock market investors are showing rare caution, choosing not to 'buy the dip' despite a 3% market fall. The Nifty 50 faces potential declines toward the 23,800 level as the Iran-Israel conflict threatens crude supplies and energy prices. A sharp drop in call option values relative to puts reflects subdued sentiment and lingering global uncertainty.

articles.headline: RBI Proposes Shared Liability Framework for Small Digital Payment Frauds
articles.summary: The Reserve Bank of India has proposed a framework to partially compensate victims of digital payment frauds up to ₹50,000, even if the customer was partly negligent. Victims could receive up to 85% of the loss or ₹25,000, whichever is lower. The cost would be shared between the RBI, the issuing bank, and the receiving bank to provide quicker relief to cyber fraud victims.

articles.headline: West Asia Conflict Disrupts India’s Hopes for Wheat Export Return
articles.summary: Escalating tensions in West Asia may stall India’s return to the global wheat market despite an expected record harvest of 120 million tonnes. Exporters are wary of shipping logistics and volatile freight rates. Traders are likely to redirect supplies to nearby markets like Bangladesh and Nepal as transportation costs rise and regional stability remains uncertain.

articles.headline: WTO Ministerial Meeting to Address Farm Issues and Fisheries Subsidies
articles.summary: Trade ministers from 166 countries will meet in Yaounde, Cameroon, for the 14th WTO Ministerial Conference. Key topics include dispute settlement reforms, agriculture-related matters, fisheries subsidies, and the e-commerce moratorium. The Indian delegation will be led by Piyush Goyal, as global trade remains disrupted by West Asia tensions and US-China tariff issues.

articles.headline: Women Now Comprise Nearly 25% of Unique NSE Investors
articles.summary: The share of female individual investors on the National Stock Exchange has risen to nearly 25%, up from 22.5% in FY23. Smaller states and the north-east are leading the trend, with Goa reaching a 33.2% female participation rate. Experts attribute the growth to digital access, rising literacy, and a shift in household savings from traditional assets to market-linked investments.

articles.headline: Oil Market Chaos Deepens as UAE and Kuwait Reduce Output
articles.summary: The UAE and Kuwait have started cutting oil production as storage fills up while tankers avoid the Strait of Hormuz. Global benchmark Brent crude has climbed toward $100 per barrel following the blockage of the vital waterway. The US is considering strategic petroleum reserve releases or naval escorts to stabilize prices, while import-dependent Asian markets face immediate supply pain.

articles.headline: Aluminium Prices Surge 9.7% as Middle East War Snarls Shipments
articles.summary: Aluminium recorded its biggest weekly gain since 2023, climbing nearly 10% on the London Metal Exchange. The US-Israeli attacks on Iran have halted metal flows through the Strait of Hormuz, causing production disruptions in the Persian Gulf. Regional premiums for delivery have hit record highs, prompting the global industry to consider how to plug emerging supply gaps.

articles.headline: Iraq Oil Output Plummets by 60% Due to Lack of Tankers
articles.summary: Iraq’s daily oil production has collapsed to 1.7 million barrels, down from 4.3 million before the Iran war. The effective closure of the Strait of Hormuz has left the country with an ever-dwindling number of tankers available for exports. Iraq is the first major Gulf producer to see a massive output cut, followed by recent reductions in the UAE and Kuwait as storage capacity reaches its limit.

articles.headline: Prolonged West Asia War Threatens Indian Fertilizer Production
articles.summary: Top Indian fertilizer supplier FACT has warned that a prolonged conflict in West Asia could disrupt raw material imports. The company relies on rock phosphate and phosphoric acid imported via sea routes from the Middle East. While India currently has sufficient stock for the non-harvest season, a continuation of hostilities beyond July could lead to supply shortages for Indian farmers.

articles.headline: Income Tax department surveys 70 restaurants across 45 cities
articles.summary: I-T officials launched a pan-India survey of approximately 70 restaurant locations for alleged tax evasion. The probe focuses on the suspected use of manipulated billing software to suppress sales figures and lower tax payments. Surveys were conducted in major cities including Delhi, Mumbai, and Bengaluru.

articles.headline: Karnataka to launch online employment platform within six months
articles.summary: The state government announced plans to establish an online employment exchange platform to match job seekers with private sector vacancies. The portal will be managed by the labour department and aims to provide a centralized solution for candidates and employers across various industries.
