articles.headline: Russia remains India's top crude oil source despite US sanctions
articles.summary: India's imports of Russian crude oil remained robust in early 2026, with Russia holding over 30% of the market share. Despite ongoing US pressure and sanctions on Russian tankers, India continues to prioritize energy security, though officials noted the share has slightly decreased as imports from other regions fluctuate.

articles.headline: China Invests Heavily in Brain-Computer Interface Technology Research
articles.summary: Beijing is prioritizing the development of Brain-Computer Interface (BCI) technology, viewing it as a critical frontier for future competition. The government is funding multiple research projects to integrate human intelligence with machine learning for medical and industrial applications. This move follows similar advancements by US-based firms like Neuralink.

articles.headline: Shipping noise forces Arabian Sea dolphins to change vocal language
articles.summary: A new study reveals that dolphins in the Arabian Sea are simplifying their vocal signals to be heard over the noise of merchant ships. Researchers found that as underwater traffic noise increases, the complexity and frequency of dolphin whistles decrease, potentially impacting their ability to hunt and navigate.

articles.headline: Antarctic microbe shows promise for waste management and environmental cleanup
articles.summary: Scientists in Pune are studying a red-pigmented microbe from an Antarctic lake that exhibits high resistance to UV radiation and toxic chemicals. The bacterium has a unique genome that could be leveraged for bioremediation of industrial pollutants and the production of sustainable biofuels.

articles.headline: Seven foreign universities to launch partnership programs in Mumbai
articles.summary: Seven international universities, including the University of Bristol and UNSW, have entered into a partnership with an edtech firm to launch degree programs in Mumbai. The initiative involves an investment of Rs 300 crore and will allow students to study for foreign degrees at a campus in Navi Mumbai.

articles.headline: NMC introduces stricter norms to curb under-resourced medical colleges
articles.summary: The National Medical Commission has tightened financial and infrastructure regulations for new and existing medical colleges. The move aims to prevent the proliferation of institutes that lack adequate staff and facilities. Colleges will now be required to maintain a corpus fund and undergo more frequent inspections.

articles.headline: Mojtaba Khamenei Likely to Succeed Late Iran Supreme Leader Amid War
articles.summary: Mojtaba Khamenei, son of the late Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, is expected to succeed him. Reports from Tehran, amid a widening regional war and a week after the Supreme Leader's reported death in an Israeli air strike, suggest the Assembly of Experts has finalized his succession. While official confirmation is pending, US President Trump stated any new leader needs US approval. Mojtaba holds significant influence in Iran's security apparatus.

articles.headline: Centre Seeks Report on Alleged Protocol Breach During President Murmu's Visit
articles.summary: The Centre's Home Ministry sought a report from West Bengal over alleged protocol breaches during President Murmu's Siliguri visit. Concerns arose from the absence of the CM, chief secretary, DGP, and lack of facilities at her arrival. PM Modi accused the TMC government of insulting the Constitution. While Mamata Banerjee denied intentional breach, the state clarified events were private, not official functions.

articles.headline: Nitish Kumar's Son Nishant Joins JD(U), Fuels Succession Speculation
articles.summary: Nishant Kumar, son of Bihar CM Nitish Kumar, an engineering graduate, officially joined the JD(U) in Patna, entering active politics after years away. His induction follows Nitish Kumar's nomination to the Rajya Sabha and comes ahead of critical government formation meetings. Nishant aims to strengthen the party and continue his father's two-decade development work, sparking speculation about a leadership transition within the state.

articles.headline: Jharkhand Father Carries Newborn in Box After Alleged Ambulance Denial
articles.summary: In West Singhbhum, Jharkhand, Ramkrishna Hembram was forced to carry his dead newborn 20km in a cardboard box after a government hospital allegedly denied an ambulance. He claimed staff pressured him to remove the body quickly, though hospital authorities denied any request was made. Following viral videos, the state's chief secretary ordered a district inquiry into the alleged negligence.

articles.headline: India Retains T20 World Cup Title, Wins Third Crown Against New Zealand
articles.summary: India made history, retaining the T20 World Cup title for a second consecutive time and securing their third overall, defeating New Zealand by 96 runs in the Ahmedabad final. India posted a record 255/5, with Sanju Samson scoring 89 (46 balls), supported by Abhishek Sharma's 18-ball fifty and Ishan Kishan. Jasprit Bumrah's 4/15 dismantled the Kiwi chase. Samson was Player of the Tournament, Bumrah Player of the Match.

articles.headline: Lakshya Sen Finishes Runner-Up at All England to Taiwan's Lin Chun-Yi
articles.summary: Indian shuttler Lakshya Sen finished runner-up in the All England men's singles final, losing in straight games (15-21, 20-22) to Taiwan's Lin Chun-Yi. Sen, exhausted after a marathon semi-final, lost a 57-minute match with high-intensity rallies. This is Sen's second runner-up finish; his performance was praised for resilience. Lin Chun-Yi became the first Chinese Taipei player to win the men's singles crown.

articles.headline: Russell Wins Chaotic Australian GP, Leads Mercedes 1-2
articles.summary: Mercedes driver George Russell won the chaotic F1 season-opening Australian Grand Prix in Melbourne, leading a Mercedes 1-2 finish with rookie Kimi Antonelli. The dramatic race featured multiple safety car periods. Ferrari's Charles Leclerc finished third. Red Bull's Max Verstappen retired due to mechanical failure. World champion Lando Norris came fifth, while home favorite Oscar Piastri failed to start after a pre-race crash.

articles.headline: Arnya-Supreme Fund Raises ₹1,030 Cr for Mumbai, Pune Residential Projects
articles.summary: Arnya Real Estates Fund Advisors and Supreme Universal have achieved the first close for their equity fund at ₹1,030 crore. The capital, primarily from domestic family offices, HNWIs, and selective offshore investors (Singapore, West Asia), will be deployed in residential projects, specifically society redevelopment projects in Mumbai and Pune, aiming to capitalize on the scalability of Mumbai's redevelopment segment.

articles.headline: Air India Adds 78 Flights for Europe, North America Amid Gulf Crisis
articles.summary: Air India is adding 78 extra weekly flights to Europe and North America between March 10 and 18, providing 17,660 additional seats. This move aims to support travelers facing cancellations due to disruptions from the ongoing Gulf/West Asia regional conflict, which constrains commercial aviation, and to meet high travel demand. The airline will utilize safe alternative routes to ensure connectivity to key destinations like London, New York, and Frankfurt.

articles.headline: India Boosts Chip Autonomy, Taps Global Indian Talent for Design
articles.summary: Niti Aayog member Vijay Kumar Saraswat announced India's pursuit of semiconductor autonomy, aiming for full independence in microchip design, production, and critical mineral supply chains. Under phase two of the India Semiconductor Mission, the strategy involves attracting Indian professionals from global MNCs to build ab initio design capabilities. The focus is on complete system design, not just components, to fortify industrial and military power and scale domestic equipment manufacturing.
